name: El Far Loop from Sant Martí Sacalm
name_original: Santuario del Far de Susqueda - trail run circular en Sant Martí Sacalm
description: The El Far Loop from Sant Martí Sacalm is a difficult 6.9-mile jogging route with 1411 feet of gain, offering panoramic views over Collsacab
region_intro: Jogging routes around Susqueda traverse a rich natural environment nestled between the Guilleries Mountains and the Collsacabra Massif. The landscape features abundant vegetation, impressive cliffs, and significant water bodies, including the prominent Susqueda Reservoir. Runners can expect varied terrain, from lush, hilly surroundings to mountainous paths with notable elevation changes. Best jogging routes around Susqueda The most popular jogging route is Salto de Sallent y Sant Joan de Fàbregues - circular trail run from Rupit, a 4.1 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a scenic circular path through the natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local runners is Els gorgs de Planes d'Hostoles - running circular through the Brugent River Protected Natural Area, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. This route winds through the protected natural area, offering diverse scenery along the Brugent River. Local runners also love the El Far Loop from Sant Martí Sacalm, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging around Susqueda is defined by the Guilleries Mountains, the Collsacabra Massif, and the Susqueda Reservoir, with paths winding through forests and along cliffs. The network offers options for all ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging difficult routes. The routes in Susqueda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 7 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Susqueda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Susqueda?
A: There are over 95 running routes available around Susqueda, offering a wide variety of options for all skill levels. These trails traverse diverse landscapes, from the tranquil shores of the Susqueda Reservoir to the challenging mountainous paths of the Guilleries Mountains and Collsacabra Massif.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Susqueda?
A: The terrain around Susqueda is quite varied. You can expect paths winding through lush forests, along the hilly surroundings of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5566896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susqueda Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and up to impressive cliffs and escarpments. Some sections, especially around the reservoir, can be rocky and steep, while others offer smoother, more accessible paths. The region is defined by the Guilleries Mountains and the Collsacabra Massif, ensuring a mix of elevations.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Susqueda?
A: Yes, Susqueda offers routes suitable for beginners. While many trails feature significant elevation changes, there are also easier paths available. For example, some sections around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5566896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susqueda Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; provide more accessible terrain for those new to trail running or looking for a less strenuous jog.

Q: What are some longer distance running options in the Susqueda area?
A: For those seeking longer distances, Susqueda has several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10489876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Lena – Sant Roc Hermitage loop from Amer&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 9.1-mile (14.6 km) route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14971097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Pool – Can Poetí Gorge loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols&lt;/a&gt; covers 8.8 miles (14.2 km) through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or landmarks to see while running in Susqueda?
A: Absolutely! Running in Susqueda offers views of several unique natural features and landmarks. You can explore the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5566896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susqueda Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the dramatic cliffs of the Guilleries Mountains, and even the historical remnants of the submerged village of Susqueda, with the visible bell tower of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2679945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Sant Romà de Sau&lt;/a&gt; when water levels are low. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/672984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osor Road&lt;/a&gt; is also noted for its spectacular architectural arches.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic viewpoints in Susqueda?
A: Yes, Susqueda is known for its spectacular vistas. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962108907/el-far-loop-from-sant-marti-sacalm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Far Loop from Sant Martí Sacalm&lt;/a&gt; lead through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views from high points like El Far (1,123 meters) and Sant Benet (1,151 meters).

Q: Are there any running trails that pass by waterfalls or unique water features?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963336612/els-gorgs-de-planes-d-hostoles-running-circular-through-the-brugent-river-protected-natural-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els gorgs de Planes d'Hostoles - running circular through the Brugent River Protected Natural Area&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, winding through a protected area with diverse scenery along the Brugent River. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4391819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí dels Murris Pool&lt;/a&gt;, a notable waterfall in the area.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Susqueda?
A: Many of the running routes in Susqueda are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955827597/salto-de-sallent-y-sant-joan-de-fabregues-circular-trail-run-from-rupit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Sallent y Sant Joan de Fàbregues - circular trail run from Rupit&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop through natural surroundings.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Susqueda?
A: The running routes in Susqueda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 7 reviews. Runners often praise the stunning natural beauty, the varied terrain that keeps runs interesting, and the sense of tranquility found amidst the Guilleries Mountains and the Susqueda Reservoir.

Q: Is Susqueda a good destination for family-friendly jogging?
A: Susqueda offers a range of trails, and while some are challenging, there are also easier, more accessible paths that can be suitable for families. Look for routes with less elevation gain and smoother surfaces, particularly those closer to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5566896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susqueda Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, for a more family-friendly running experience.

Q: Are running trails in Susqueda dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in natural areas like Susqueda are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to check local regulations or specific trail signs. Ensure your dog is well-behaved, kept on a leash where required, and that you carry waste bags to maintain the cleanliness of the natural environment.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Susqueda?
A: Susqueda's rich natural environment makes it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are advisable, while winters are milder but can have cooler, wetter conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink near the running routes in Susqueda?
A: While the focus of Susqueda is its natural environment, you can find amenities in nearby villages. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/683536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupit Village&lt;/a&gt;, often a starting point for some routes, offers options for refreshments and meals after your run.
tips:
The Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Lighthouse from the 11th century, is located at 1,112 m high, with magnificent views over the Susqueda marsh, the cliffs of Tavertet, Les Guilleries and El Montseny, leaving the village of Sant Martí Sacalm at his feet The sanctuary has an inn, restaurant and picnic area.

Located at the tip of a large cliff, with wide views in all directions, which is undoubtedly where its name comes from, the lighthouse sanctuary is one of the most visible places in the country. Now as before or even more since the modern restoration, the sanctuary to the Virgin of the Lighthouse, is the most visited and well-known place in the whole area. The oldest news about the Lighthouse dates back to 1256 and it was already recorded as a place of great devotion in 1332. It presides over its church, all stone and barrel vaulted, rebuilt after the earthquakes of the 15th century, a devout Gothic image of alabaster, restored and again polychrome in 1922. The church forms a complex with a large building or inn, former hermits' house, largely rebuilt between 1599 and 1648.
Restaurant and bar to rest with good views
Small hermitage halfway to the lighthouse sanctuary. At this point begins the degree of Sta. Anna that allows us access to St. Martí Sacalm. Some sections are not cyclable (depends on the skill)

faqs.question: Where does the El Far Loop from Sant Martí Sacalm trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts in the village of Sant Martí Sacalm. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's common for trails originating from villages to have designated parking areas or roadside parking available near the trailhead. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the El Far Loop from Sant Martí Sacalm dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a challenging experience through varied terrain. While dogs are generally allowed on trails in this region, it's important to consider the trail's difficulty. With steep ascents, descents, and narrow paths near precipices, it might be demanding for some dogs. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are fit enough for the strenuous sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to experience this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for humid ground on steep sections, spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit. The weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery, including the oak forests, is particularly beautiful. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially icy conditions on higher, exposed parts.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Collsacabra area or this trail?
faqs.answer: As of now, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails in the Collsacabra area, including the El Far Loop. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or less experienced runners?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as difficult for jogging. It features steep ascents and descents, narrow paths, and sections close to precipices. Some parts are described as "very strong" and "very vertical." It requires good to very good fitness, sturdy shoes, and sure-footedness. It is generally not recommended for beginners or individuals with vertigo.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Far Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and demanding. You'll encounter narrow and winding paths, forest tracks, and unpaved segments. Expect steep ascents and descents, with some sections having humid ground. The route navigates ancient "graus" (paths) along impressive cliffs, and some stretches are very close to precipices. While some parts may be paved, others can have loose gravel.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Santuario del Far and its adjacent viewpoint, offering breathtaking panoramic views, especially over the Guilleries massif. You'll also pass through picturesque oak forests and can enjoy magnificent vistas from various points. Other religious buildings like the Church of Sant Martí Sacalm and the Hermitage of Santa Anna are also highlights.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the El Far Loop?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this 11.1 km (6.9 mi) loop typically takes around 1 hour and 33 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary significantly based on your fitness level, pace, and how often you stop to enjoy the views or navigate challenging sections.

faqs.question: Does the El Far Loop intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sant Benet-Mare de Déu del Far, El Camí IT7TR4 El Camí de Llevant: de Montserrat a Salses, and [CIMA GI12] Condreu - El Far, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places for refreshments along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, a notable feature is the bar located at the Santuario del Far. This provides a great opportunity for refreshments and a break while enjoying the spectacular views.

faqs.question: Are there any sections of the trail that require extra caution?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The trail includes segments that are very close to precipices, particularly along the ancient "graus" that navigate the cliffs. These sections require significant caution and sure-footedness. The route is explicitly not recommended for individuals with vertigo due to these exposed parts.
